design_philosophy.core_loop: Select golfer with unique attributes, navigate two distinct courses by managing aim, power, and hazard avoidance across multiple holes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision aiming and power control vs. hazard placement (sand traps, water, rough, trees); character selection creates risk-reward tension between control ease and range capability
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic golf simulation with celebrity endorsement (Lee Trevino); mastery of realistic course layouts
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately asymmetric character design forcing meaningful strategic choice: Pretty Amy offers easiest control but shortest range; Big Jumbo offers maximum distance but poorest precision. No middle-ground 'optimal' character—player must commit to a playstyle. Course design philosophy differs between regions: US course emphasizes bunker avoidance (punishment), Japan course emphasizes tight boundaries and water management (precision).

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection determines difficulty baseline: choosing Big Jumbo creates execution-heavy precision demands; Pretty Amy reduces precision requirements. Course selection (US vs Japan) also provides difficulty variance.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Two courses available: United States course (bunker-heavy, based on PGA West Stadium Course) and Japan course (water hazard and boundary-focused). Likely progression through holes on selected course.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct golfers with meaningfully different control/range trade-offs
Two thematically different course designs (US bunker-focused vs Japan precision-focused)
Character-specific difficulty curves encourage trying alternative golfers

world_building_techniques: Geographic differentiation: US course vs Japan course with distinct hazard philosophies
Photorealistic ending shot (country club lodge at sunset with trees) provides environmental narrative closure
Celebrity endorsement (Lee Trevino as 'Super Mex' in NA version) grounds game in real golf culture

faction_indicators: Regional course identity: US (bunker mastery) vs Japan (precision/boundary management) may signal playstyle preference or national gaming culture affiliation
danger_signals: Character selection publicly declares playstyle commitment: Big Jumbo (aggressive distance play) vs Pretty Amy (conservative precision play)
environmental_storytelling: PGA West Stadium Course recreation grounds game in real-world golf prestige
US vs Japan course design reflects regional golf course aesthetic philosophy
Sunset country club lodge ending provides aspirational leisure-class framing
